clauses.rationale: This clause only identifies the parties and rental unit; none of the candidate rules apply.
The fixed-term tenancy dates do not address or conflict with any of the candidate rules.
The clause only states the monthly rent amount and due date; it does not conflict with any of the provided rules.
The deposit is limited to one month's rent and is applied only to the final rental period, consistent with ON-DEPOSIT-CAP and ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a security and damage deposit, which is expressly prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a non-refundable pet deposit, which is expressly prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause expressly prohibits all pets and animals in the unit and on the property, violating ON-NO-PET-BAN.
The clause permits only a refundable key deposit and caps it at the actual replacement cost of the keys, which is consistent with ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires landlord permission for overnight guests and charges a per-night guest fee, both prohibited by ON-NO-GUEST-BAN.

The clause permits entry at any time without prior notice for inspections or maintenance, contrary to ON-ENTRY-NOTICE, which generally requires at least 24 hours’ written notice and entry between 8 a.m. and 8 p.m.
The clause concerns the tenant's responsibility for cleanliness and does not conflict with any of the candidate rules.
The clause assigns responsibility for damage caused by the tenant or guests but does not impose a prohibited deposit or otherwise conflict with any candidate rule.
The clause imposes a $75 NSF administration charge, exceeding the $20 maximum permitted by ON-NSF-CAP (excluding any separate bank charges).
The clause requires the tenant to provide post-dated cheques, which is prohibited by ON-NO-POSTDATED; this payment method may only be chosen voluntarily.

The clause permits rent increases at any time with only 30 days' notice, contrary to ON-RENT-INCREASE-NOTICE, which requires at least 90 days' written notice and generally limits increases to once every 12 months.
The clause states that no interest is payable on any deposit, conflicting with ON-DEPOSIT-INTEREST, which requires annual interest on the rent deposit.
The clause merely makes tenant contents insurance optional and does not conflict with any of the candidate rules.
The clause imposes an absolute ban on assignment or subletting, contrary to ON-SUBLET-CONSENT, which permits these with landlord consent and prohibits unreasonable withholding of consent.
The clause concerns the landlord’s repair and maintenance obligations and does not conflict with or trigger any of the listed candidate rules.
The clause permits the landlord to deliberately shut off electricity, heat, or water for unpaid rent, which directly violates ON-VITAL-SERVICES.
The clause violates ON-LOCK-CHANGE because it allows the landlord to change the locks without giving the tenant replacement keys while rent arrears remain unpaid.
None of the candidate rules addresses smoking restrictions, so the clause does not violate any provided rule.

The clause only requires the tenant to establish and pay for electricity service directly; it does not authorize the landlord to withhold or deliberately interfere with electricity under ON-VITAL-SERVICES. None of the other candidate rules apply.
This severability clause does not conflict with any of the provided candidate rules.
